steps.title: Find the cause and result
steps.content: The first sentence says every chemical compound has a unique pattern of reflected light. The next sentence describes Rossi analyzing reflected light on Ceres and determining that the regions contain sodium carbonate.

steps.title: Choose the causal transition
steps.content: Choice A signals contrast despite some condition, choice B signals simultaneity, and choice D signals comparison. The remaining transition shows that Rossi's identification follows from the spectroscopic-fingerprint principle introduced in the previous sentence. The answer is C.
